title: acceptEdits auto-approves an ORDINARY nested in-project write -> allow (control isolating that a/c/d's denial is protected-path, not nesting)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/deep/OK.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: acceptEdits auto-approves ordinary nested in-project writes with no prompt (creating intermediate dirs as needed): engine=allow, canUseTool does NOT fire. This is the control proving a/c/d's protected-path denials are a protected-path rule, not a nesting/depth limitation. Only the leading path segment being a protected dir (.git/.claude/.vscode) flips allow -> ask.
